reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser image-to-text pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls pipe(imageURL, {max_new_tokens, streamer}) on the image-to-text pipeline for a VisionEncoderDecoder (ViT encoder → RoBERTa-style decoder); a TextStreamer posts each decoded token to the page with a timestamp. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). The final text is out.generated_text from the real generate() loop; tokens are streamed live via a real TextStreamer(pipe.tokenizer) — the See-inside per-token timing is genuine, not simulated. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); Token-by-token streaming via TextStreamer with skip_prompt/skip_special_tokens; each token is posted with a t offset for the live trace.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a single line of printed text transcribed from a cropped image region. TrOCR reads ONE line, so the page lets you draw a crop box; the transcription forms token-by-token and the See-inside shows per-token timing. Output is text (with a Copy button), plus the crop canvas.
rubric.notes: trocr-small-printed is a single-LINE, printed-text OCR model — not full-page or handwriting. The manual crop step is the honest way to feed it one line; multi-line documents need repeated crops. This is a real model limitation surfaced by the crop UI.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/trocr-small-printed, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (image-to-text), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~185 MB) and license (MIT). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'Xenova/trocr-small-printed', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:185, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/trocr-small-printed so the ~185 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("OCR failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.
rubric.notes: Streaming shows the transcription forming; a Copy button appears once done.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: file dropzone (role=button), sample thumbnails (role=button), a draggable crop selection on <canvas id=canvas aria-label='Selected image — drag to box the line of text to read'>, a Reset-selection chip, a max-tokens range slider, a Read-text button and a Copy-text chip.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — the transcription is the real generate() output streamed token-by-token; the timing trace is measured per token. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

The crop canvas carries a descriptive aria-label; sample thumbnails have alt text; Reset/Read/Copy chips and buttons have names; the max-tokens slider is labelled; status uses role=status aria-live; the transcription is selectable text. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); generate() + token streaming run in the worker; tokens arrive incrementally so the page updates progressively rather than blocking on the full decode. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive technology overlay boxes
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name and a text equivalent so assistive tech, translation and reading mode can reach it.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the crop canvas carries an aria-label explaining the drag-to-box interaction; the decoded transcription is real selectable text (not locked in the canvas).

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the VisionEncoderDecoder generate() runs in worker.js and streams tokens incrementally via TextStreamer, so the UI updates progressively and never blocks on the full decode.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live, labelled crop canvas + max-tokens slider, named Reset/Read/Copy controls.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex/grid; the crop canvas is inline-size:100% so it never overflows.

openQuestions: Single-line printed OCR only — is an additive line-segmentation step (auto-crop each line) worth building so full pages work without manual cropping?

No fresh measured run captured here; a headless run citing a real transcription (e.g. on sample-total.png) + latency would let real-inference score 5. Sub-24px tap targets also need manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless run citing a real transcription + per-token timing so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Add an additive line-segmentation front-end so multi-line documents can be OCR'd without manual per-line crops.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for trocr-ocr (Xenova/trocr-small-printed, image-to-text, q8, ~185 MB, MIT). A real VisionEncoderDecoder OCR run off-thread with live token-by-token streaming (TextStreamer) and a genuine per-token timing trace; a draggable crop canvas feeds the model one printed line. Text output with Copy, shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest weaknesses: single-LINE printed OCR only — multi-line needs repeated crops (io-shape 4/minor); no fresh measured run captured here; sub-24px tap-target flags at needs-review.
